other_observers: Many observers over the time period the bird was seen. There were about 12 different people there while I was watching the bird.
duration: about half of the time over 50 minutes
description:
Behavior - Bird
was landing in a leafless bush and ornamental tree when it would arrive back in
the area. It would then fly on or near the ground looking for seeds or hiding.
I couldn’t see it through the vegetation when it dropped down so not 100% sure
what it did. One time it flew two houses away to their back yard bird seed
feeder. That is where we all got good looks and photos without going into the
yard. Plumage - Mostly medium gray cardinal shaped
bird with a crest. The following areas had red including crest tip, around bill
and eyes, outer tail feathers, primary edges, and tibial feathers. There was a
light thin reddish/pink wash down the center of the chest and belly but not as prominent
as in breeding season. That is why I thought it might be a juvenile male verses
an adult male but I’m not sure about that. The bill was short, stubby, thick,
and yellow. Vocalizations – As I was arriving in
the area, I wasn’t sure I was in the right place. No other birders were there.
The first bird I heard vocalizing was the loud metallic chip call note of the
Pyrrhuloxia. I didn’t find it right away but could hear it. I soon found it. I
did record an audio clip that isn’t very good as everyone at the time was
talking excitedly about seeing the bird. Oh well! I never heard a song, only
the call notes.
photographer: Photographer: Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe, Sue Riffe
